name: Sofia
description: Analog formant oscillator implementing extended FOF synthesis. Triangle core plus two decaying-sine ripple elements (A, B) produce phase-coherent vocal/organic tones without filters. Per-element ratio, damp, warp, shape, and tracking give wide spectral control.

controls.name: PITCH
controls.description: Continuous pitch over a two-octave range. Sums with OCTAVE switch and all pitch CV inputs.
controls.detail: ±12 semitones · fundamental range ~0.4Hz–12kHz combined with OCTAVE

controls.name: OCTAVE
controls.description: 8-position rotary selecting octave band. LO enters LFO/subsonic territory.

controls.name: ELEMENTS MIX
controls.description: Balance between ripple elements A and B. Noon = 1:1.
controls.detail: CCW = A only · noon = equal · CW = B only · CV ±5V offset

controls.name: FUND·ELEM MIX
controls.description: Balance between the fundamental (base) tone and the summed ripple elements at MAIN OUT.
controls.detail: min = fundamental only · max = elements only · CV ±5V offset

controls.name: PITCH FM attenuator
controls.description: Attenuator for the PITCH FM input. Modulates the whole oscillator including ripples — preserves waveform shape.
controls.detail: 0–10 · shape-preserving FM

controls.name: GLOBAL FM attenuator
controls.description: Attenuator for GLOBAL FM input. Modulates only the base tone — preserves overall spectrum, ripples stay put.
controls.detail: 0–10 · spectrum-preserving FM

controls.name: RATIO A
controls.description: Density of ripple A relative to the fundamental — sets the spectral peak / formant position. Min ≈ no ripple, max ≈ 8 octaves above fundamental.
controls.detail: 0–10 · ~1 to ~240 ripples per cycle

controls.name: RATIO B
controls.description: Density of ripple B relative to the fundamental — second formant peak. Same scaling as RATIO A.
controls.detail: 0–10 · ~1 to ~240 ripples per cycle

controls.name: RATIO A CV attenuator
controls.description: Bipolar attenuator for RATIO A CV input.
controls.detail: 0–10 · for ±5V CV

controls.name: RATIO B CV attenuator
controls.description: Bipolar attenuator for RATIO B CV input.
controls.detail: 0–10 · for ±5V CV

controls.name: DAMP A (slider)
controls.description: Decay rate of ripple A — behaves like Q/resonance (narrow spike at bottom, long ring at top) but is not a filter.
controls.detail: 0 = narrow spike ~1% period · 100 = nearly undamped

controls.name: DAMP B (slider)
controls.description: Decay rate of ripple B.
controls.detail: 0 = narrow spike ~1% period · 100 = nearly undamped

controls.name: WARP A
controls.description: Bends density distribution of ripple A across the cycle. Noon = uniform.
controls.detail: CCW = density rises toward end of cycle · CW = density front-loaded

controls.name: WARP B
controls.description: Bends density distribution of ripple B across the cycle.
controls.detail: CCW = density rises toward end of cycle · CW = density front-loaded

controls.name: RATIO TRACK A
controls.description: Miniature switch. Tracks element A's ripple density to pitch or keeps it fixed.
controls.detail: track = constant waveform shape · off = constant spectrum (formant)

controls.name: DAMP TRACK A
controls.description: Miniature switch. Tracks element A's decay rate to pitch or keeps it fixed.
controls.detail: track = constant waveform · off = constant decay time

controls.name: RATIO TRACK B
controls.description: Miniature switch. Tracks element B's ripple density to pitch.
controls.detail: track = constant shape · off = constant spectrum

controls.name: DAMP TRACK B
controls.description: Miniature switch. Tracks element B's decay to pitch.
controls.detail: track = constant shape · off = constant decay time

controls.name: SHAPE A
controls.description: Per-element switch between sine-like and square-like ripple.
controls.detail: SINE = cleaner tone · SQUARE = richer spectrum, more aggressive

controls.name: SHAPE B
controls.description: Per-element switch between sine-like and square-like ripple.
controls.detail: SINE = cleaner tone · SQUARE = richer spectrum, more aggressive

inputs.name: PITCH CV V/OCT
inputs.description: 1V/octave pitch input, accepts voltages from -10V to +10V. Usable range depends on manual OCTAVE/PITCH settings.

inputs.name: PITCH FM
inputs.description: Pitch FM that modulates fundamental and ripples together — preserves waveform shape.
inputs.voltage: audio rate capable · scaled by attenuator

inputs.name: GLOBAL FM
inputs.description: FM that shifts only the base tone — preserves the overall spectral shape of the ripples.
inputs.voltage: audio rate capable · scaled by attenuator

inputs.name: RATIO A
inputs.description: CV for ripple A density. Scaled by its attenuator.

inputs.name: RATIO B
inputs.description: CV for ripple B density. Scaled by its attenuator.

inputs.name: DAMP A
inputs.description: CV offsetting ripple A decay rate.

inputs.name: DAMP B
inputs.description: CV offsetting ripple B decay rate.

inputs.name: WARP A
inputs.description: CV offsetting ripple A density warping across the cycle.

inputs.name: WARP B
inputs.description: CV offsetting ripple B density warping.

inputs.name: IMPULSE A
inputs.description: Individual decay envelope for element A — the raw amplitude curve without ripples. Useful for self-patching and triggering external gear.

inputs.name: IMPULSE B
inputs.description: Individual decay envelope for element B.

outputs.name: MAIN OUT
outputs.description: Final mix of the fundamental and the two ripple elements per the MIX controls.

outputs.name: FUND OUT
outputs.description: Pure sinusoidal fundamental, unaffected by the triangle-core saturation.

outputs.name: A OUT
outputs.description: Element A ripple at full amplitude, without its decay envelope applied.

outputs.name: B OUT
outputs.description: Element B ripple at full amplitude, without its decay envelope applied.

outputs.name: IMPULSE A OUT
outputs.description: Decay envelope of element A, without the ripple carrier. Pulse-like shape spawning each cycle.

outputs.name: IMPULSE B OUT
outputs.description: Decay envelope of element B, without the ripple carrier.

behaviors.name: FOF formant synthesis — time-domain formants
behaviors.description: Each cycle of the fundamental spawns a pair of decaying sine/square bursts (elements A and B). RATIO sets ripple density ≈ formant centre frequency, DAMP sets decay rate ≈ formant bandwidth. Because both elements are derived from the core, the spectrum stays strictly harmonic even with 'detuned-looking' ripples.

behaviors.name: Two independent formants (A vs B)
behaviors.trigger: distinct RATIO A vs RATIO B
behaviors.description: Classic vocal vowel approximation uses two formants spaced asymmetrically. Set RATIO A to a low ratio (F1) and RATIO B higher (F2), adjust ELEMENTS MIX for balance. Different vowel shapes emerge from distinct RATIO pairs.

behaviors.name: Ratio tracking vs fixed-formant
behaviors.description: Track ON → ripple density scales with fundamental, so the waveform shape is constant across pitch (sounds like a timbre). Track OFF → ripple frequency stays fixed, formant stays fixed in Hz — the hallmark of vocal/acoustic-sounding pitched playback.

behaviors.name: Damp tracking vs fixed decay
behaviors.description: Track ON → decay time scales with pitch (constant shape). Track OFF → decay time stays fixed in seconds, so at high pitches each cycle rings over the next, blurring toward a sustained resonance; at low pitches the ripple fully decays within a cycle.

behaviors.name: Shape switch — sine vs square ripple
behaviors.trigger: SHAPE A or SHAPE B switch
behaviors.description: Per-element selection of the ripple carrier. Sine is smoother and vocal; square adds odd harmonics producing more aggressive / buzzy / reed-like tones.

behaviors.name: Warp reshapes density across the cycle
behaviors.description: Bends the ripple density distribution within one fundamental period. CW front-loads the density (strong attack, sparse tail); CCW pushes density to the end (sparse front, dense tail). Creates asymmetric articulation — plucks, bowed gestures, breathy attacks.

behaviors.name: Pitch FM preserves waveform shape
behaviors.description: All elements follow modulation together. Audio-rate FM produces classic FM sidebands around the fundamental; the ripple density tracks so the formant shape remains coherent.

behaviors.name: Global FM preserves spectral shape
behaviors.description: Only the base tone responds, ripples stay at their current frequencies. Yields formant-locked pitch wobble — source moves under fixed resonances, like a vocal tract with a changing pitched source.

behaviors.name: Base tone mix — smooth sine to elements-only
behaviors.description: Min position: only the saturated fundamental — a warm, slightly driven sine. Max: only the ripple elements, which can be very bright. Intermediate positions emulate source+filter relationships without actually filtering anything.

behaviors.name: Self-patching for wild timbres
behaviors.trigger: cables from A/B/IMPULSE OUTs to CV inputs
behaviors.description: Manual explicitly encourages self-patching. Routing IMPULSE A → RATIO B, or A OUT → WARP B, or FUND OUT → DAMP A creates recursive internal modulation producing animal, metallic, or chaotic textures beyond static FOF.

behaviors.name: Individual outs for multitracking / FX splits
behaviors.trigger: use A OUT / B OUT / FUND OUT separately
behaviors.description: Each ripple is available at full amplitude without decay, plus IMPULSE outs give the raw decay envelopes. Route each to a different processor (delay on B, reverb on A, dry fundamental) for a pseudo-stereo spread from one oscillator.

behaviors.name: Subsonic / LFO operation
behaviors.description: Fundamental drops below 1Hz. Ripple elements become audible grains riding a slow cycle — Sofia effectively turns into a rhythmic formant generator or complex LFO cluster.

patchIdeas.name: Vocal vowel — fixed formants, played keyboard
patchIdeas.patch: SHAPE A/B = SINE · RATIO TRACK A/B = OFF (fixed formants) · RATIO A ~3 (F1), RATIO B ~6 (F2) · DAMP sliders mid · FUND·ELEM MIX mid · V/Oct from keyboard
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* OCTAVE: 4'
* RATIO A: 3
* RATIO B: 6
* RATIO TRACK A: OFF
* RATIO TRACK B: OFF
* SHAPE A: SINE
* SHAPE B: SINE

- Keyboard (CV) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Morphing vowel sequence
patchIdeas.patch: Two S&H from clocked random → RATIO A CV and RATIO B CV · attenuators ~5 · fixed formant tracking · creates stepped vowel shifts per note
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* RATIO TRACK A: OFF
* RATIO TRACK B: OFF
* DAMP A: 50
* DAMP B: 60

- Seq (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- Clock (Out) c> S&H1 (Trig)
- Clock (Out) c> S&H2 (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H1 (In)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H2 (In)
- S&H1 (Out) >> Sofia (RATIO A)
- S&H2 (Out) >> Sofia (RATIO B)
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Plucked string via warp and damp
patchIdeas.patch: SHAPE A = SQUARE · DAMP A low (short ring) · WARP A full CW (front-loaded density) · RATIO A tracks on · envelope into DAMP A CV extends ring
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* SHAPE A: SQUARE
* DAMP A: 20
* WARP A: full CW
* RATIO TRACK A: ON
* FUND·ELEM MIX: 8

- Seq (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- Env (Out) >> Sofia (DAMP A)
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-FM beast — IMPULSE into WARP
patchIdeas.patch: IMPULSE A → WARP B · IMPULSE B → RATIO A · creates recursive internal modulation, producing growling, animal-like dynamics that respond to pitch
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* RATIO A: 4
* RATIO B: 7
* DAMP A: 40
* DAMP B: 60

- Kbd (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- Sofia (IMPULSE A OUT) >> Sofia (WARP B)
- Sofia (IMPULSE B OUT) >> Sofia (RATIO A)
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate Global FM with static formants
patchIdeas.patch: External sine into GLOBAL FM attenuated · RATIO A/B fixed, TRACK OFF · fundamental wobbles under stationary formants — vowel-through-vibrato effect
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* RATIO TRACK A: OFF
* RATIO TRACK B: OFF
* RATIO A: 3
* RATIO B: 8

- Kbd (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- VCO (Sine) >> Sofia (GLOBAL FM) // low index
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Two-tap stereo spread (A vs B outs)
patchIdeas.patch: A OUT → delay L · B OUT → chorus R · FUND OUT centered dry — pseudo-stereo from one oscillator with element-specific processing
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* RATIO A: 2
* RATIO B: 5

- Kbd (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- Sofia (A OUT) -> Delay (In)
- Delay (Out) -> Mixer (ChL)
- Sofia (B OUT) -> Chorus (In)
- Chorus (Out) -> Mixer (ChR)
- Sofia (FUND OUT) -> Mixer (ChC)

patchIdeas.name: LFO / rhythmic formant pulses
patchIdeas.patch: OCTAVE = LO · fundamental ~2Hz · DAMP A very low, DAMP B high · ripples become audible bursts on each cycle — a complex pulse LFO / drone
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* OCTAVE: LO
* DAMP A: 5
* DAMP B: 80
* RATIO A: 6
* RATIO B: 9

- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Zadar-modulated formant scan
patchIdeas.patch: Zadar env → WARP A · second Zadar env → RATIO B with different shape · creates moving, breathy vocal gestures per note
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* SHAPE A: SINE
* SHAPE B: SINE
* RATIO TRACK A: OFF

- Kbd (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- Trig (Trig) t> Zadar (Trig1)
- Trig (Trig) t> Zadar (Trig2)
- Zadar (Out1) >> Sofia (WARP A)
- Zadar (Out2) >> Sofia (RATIO B)
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Reed/brass via square ripples and bright formants
patchIdeas.patch: SHAPE A/B = SQUARE · RATIO A ~4, RATIO B ~8 · DAMP B high for sustain · FUND·ELEM MIX high · breath-shaped envelope into VCA
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sofia:
* SHAPE A: SQUARE
* SHAPE B: SQUARE
* RATIO A: 4
* RATIO B: 8
* DAMP B: 80
* FUND·ELEM MIX: 8

- Kbd (Pitch) p> Sofia (PITCH CV V/OCT)
- BreathEnv (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Sofia (MAIN OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
